task_language: Pick the akita black bowl on the stove and place it on the plate
perturbation_semantics: Bowl positions swapped — the akita_black_bowl referenced is now on the stove fixture (KITCHEN frame).
strategy_notes: MULTI-ATTEMPT MODE solve. PERCEPTION: bowl on stove localized via SAM3 prompt 'the black bowl on the stove' (score 0.79) -> world (-0.30, -0.15, z~0.96-0.98). Plate localized via SAM3 prompt 'the white plate with red rim' (score 0.96) -> world (-0.21, 0.20, 0.91). KITCHEN frame (eef home z=1.17). SOLUTION: a 3-command chain — (1) move_to pre-position high over bowl at (-0.30, -0.15, 1.20) with gripper open. (2) pi0_pick prompt 'pick up the bowl' max_chunks=15, lift_thresh=0.04, gripper_closed_thresh=0.05 -> success in 12 chunks, peak_lift 0.045m, gripper qpos sum 0.015 (rim-hooked). (3) chained pi0_pick prompt 'place the bowl on the plate' max_chunks=30, lift_thresh=999, gripper_closed_thresh=0 (turns pi0_pick into a generic Pi0 driver) -> libero_terminated:true in 13 chunks. KEY INSIGHT: prior two attempts (a prior attempt, a prior attempt) discovered that scripted move_to with gripper:+1 progressively over-closes the binary OSC gripper, ejecting the rim-grasped bowl mid-carry. Solution: keep the carry inside Pi0's own controller by chaining a second pi0_pick with a place-phrase prompt — Pi0's policy controls the gripper continuously and carries+places without dropping. Prior attempts didn't try a CHAINED pi0_pick with 'place the bowl on the plate' as the second call; they tried the FULL task language or end-to-end which mistargeted destination. The short place-phrase prompt navigated Pi0 to the actual plate at (-0.21, 0.24, 0.93). Pre-positioning over the correct bowl on the stove first anchors Pi0 to the right target (vs. end-to-end from home where Pi0 misidentifies bowl).

place_result.name: pick
place_result.instruction: place the bowl on the plate
